marketState: neutral-bearish: price above fair value, storage surplus, weekly downtrend, but historically crowded speculative short
summary: Front-month Aug 2026 trades at $3.269, $0.311 above the model fair value of $2.958 but inside the $2.458–$3.458 band — leaving only $0.189 of room to the upper band edge versus $0.311 of mean-reversion downside to fair value. The 1–7D and 8–16D detailed weather demand forecasts are both null in this snapshot, which removes a key directional input; realised demand is running at 141.1 Bcf/day (+12% vs 5-year normal), but fell sharply –7.2 Bcf/day in the past 24 hours, tilting the near-term weather picture toward cooling demand softness. Seasonality is flagged as LOW reliability (44 pp 10–90 spread), the typical 4-week move is –2.3%, and although the 3-week window shows 69% probability of higher prices it flips to 31% at week 4. Storage at +153 Bcf above the 5-year average remains a persistent bearish fundamental overhang, and the weekly/daily charts both carry bearish 50<200 EMA crosses, while managed money sits at just the 17th COT percentile (net short –84,909 contracts) — a historically crowded short that is a contrarian long signal with squeeze potential on any bullish catalyst.
